title: RelSpec — declared reliability specification for PROCEDURE_RELIABLE
description: PROCEDURE_RELIABLE is not a feeling about a procedure: it is a claim about a NAMED procedure, over a DECLARED reference class, at a DECLARED risk stratum, against a DECLARED metric and threshold, under a DECLARED evaluation protocol, on a DECLARED evidence basis, declared BEFORE the outcome it is used to assess. declared_at is the timestamp that makes pre-outcome declaration checkable; the cross-record layer rejects any RelSpec declared at or after the recorded result time.

procedure_id.description: the procedure whose reliability is claimed

procedure_version.description: the edition of that procedure; reliability does not transfer across editions without argument

reference_class.description: the population over which the reliability figure is defined

risk_stratum.description: the declared risk stratum the threshold is set for

metric.description: the reliability metric (precision, sensitivity, false-negative rate, ...)

threshold.description: the value the metric must meet

evaluation_protocol.description: how the metric is measured, including sampling and blinding

evidence_basis.description: the evidence the reliability figure rests on

declared_at.description: timestamp of declaration; must precede any recorded result time (pre-outcome declaration — checked by validate_cross_record_semantics)
